steps.title: Reorganize and factor the equation
steps.content:
Starting with $$x - 29 = (x - a)(x - 29),$$ move all terms to one side and factor out the common factor $(x - 29)$:
$$x - 29 - (x - a)(x - 29) = 0$$
Factor: $$(x - 29)[1 - (x - a)] = 0$$
Simplify: $$(x - 29)(a + 1 - x) = 0$$

steps.title: Apply zero product property
steps.content:
From $(x - 29)(a + 1 - x) = 0$, either factor equals zero:
$$x - 29 = 0 \quad \text{or} \quad a + 1 - x = 0$$
This gives $x = 29$ or $x = a + 1$.

steps.title: Identify which candidates are solutions
steps.content:
The two solutions are:
  • $x = 29$ (Candidate III)
  • $x = a + 1$ (Candidate II)
Candidate I ($x = a$) is not a solution. That leaves II and III only as solutions. That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
